title: lithospermum ruderale
intro: Lithospermum ruderale, also known as Bastard Larkspur or Wild Larkspur, is a perennial herb that belongs to the borage family and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. Traditionally, Lithospermum ruderale has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it a remedy for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which provide its medicinal properties and contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Lithospermum ruderale can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal and health benefits, and it is also used in traditional herbal medicine to treat skin conditions and other ailments.
